reforms.description: The government announced the Plan d’action concerté pour l’emploi senior, aiming to increase the employment rate of 55-64 year-olds to 50% by 2010 through various measures, including the introduction of a short-term employment contract for older workers.
reforms.component_name: Employment Rate Initiative for Older Workers
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance the employability of older workers, thereby increasing their participation in the labor market and contributing to overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced the plan and initiated measures to support older workers.
reforms.importance_rationale: The plan represents a significant effort to address demographic challenges in the labor market, focusing on a specific age group and introducing new employment contracts.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced the Plan d’action concerté pour l’emploi senior which aims to increase the employment rate of 55-64 year-olds to 50% from the current 37%, by 2010.
